message:
fix(web): use structural type for HeaderAccessor instead of Pick<Headers>

The Pick<Headers, "get"> type was overly restrictive because it required
the full Headers class structure, which may differ between environments
(Node.js, Edge, browser). Some Next.js edge runtime environments provide
headers-like objects that have a get() method but aren't instanceof Headers.

Changes:
- Replace Pick<Headers, "get"> with structural type { get(name: string): string | null }
- This allows any object with a compatible get() method to satisfy the type
- Maintains type safety while improving runtime compatibility

This fixes potential TypeScript errors in Next.js middleware and API routes
that run in edge runtime where the global Headers class may differ.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
